metadata.source_file: client alarm & SLD source spreadsheet (kept outside the repo)
metadata.description: Structured capture of the client alarm + SLD register spreadsheet for the Newtown BESS. Intended as the single source of truth for replacing alarms and data points across the backend (neems-core), frontend (neems-react), and Modbus simulation (neems-rtac-sim).

conventions.alarm_num_namespacing: alarm_num is unique WITHIN a category but NOT across categories: e.g. 601 is a digital MP-1A status bit AND an analog MP-1A measurement. Always key by (category, alarm_num).
conventions.derived_fields: Any object containing "_derived": true was computed here, not taken from the spreadsheet (suggested_code_name, modbus, severity_signals, threshold). Raw spreadsheet values use *_raw keys.
conventions.modbus: Digital alarms are packed into 22 contiguous 16-bit holding registers starting at address 8. register_index is 0-based within that block; register_address = 8 + register_index. Analog points have no register assignment in the spreadsheet (TBD).
conventions.severity: The spreadsheet encodes severity indirectly via SLD color (red = critical/trip, yellow = warning), the IsFire flag, and the availability impact. These raw signals are captured under severity_signals; mapping them to a 1-5 level is left to implementation (see README).

severity_levels.1: Emergency — active fire, call 911, emergency shutdown
severity_levels.2: High — activate COF, contact SMEs, immediate response

sld_change_color_legend.red: electrical/controls fault or fire (primary object red, often flashing)
sld_change_color_legend.blue: border indication: site not ready to operate/close in (controls problem)

megapack_digital_template.note: 30 status bits per Megapack, DNP3/Modbus points 1-30 (pt_number = bit offset + 1).

megapack_analog_template.note: 30 analog measurements per Megapack, captured in spreadsheet order (offset 0-29). MP-1A is the clean copy; see data_quality_issues for mislabelings in the other blocks.
